sanskrit: कर्मेन्द्रियाणि संयम्य य आस्ते मनसा स्मरन् । इन्द्रियार्थान्विमूढात्मा मिथ्याचारः स उच्यते ॥
hindiTranslation: जो कर्मेन्द्रियों सम्पूर्ण इन्द्रियों को हठपूर्वक रोककर मनसे इन्द्रियोंके विषयोंका चिन्तन करता रहता है, वह मूढ़ बुद्धिवाला मनुष्य मिथ्याचारी मिथ्या आचरण करनेवाला कहा जाता है ॥
englishTranslation: One who restrains the organs of action but keeps thinking of sense objects in the mind is deluded and called a hypocrite.
essence: Outer restraint means nothing if the mind still feeds desire.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Holding back your actions while the mind still chases desire is self-deception. Outer restraint means little without inner discipline.
simpleInsight: It removes the comfort of pretending. Real restraint must include the mind, not just visible behavior.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen between duty and grief. Krishna sharpens the teaching: merely stopping the body while the mind clings to desire is not true renunciation. This verse exposes false restraint before showing the right way forward.
modernRelevance: You delete the app, but keep checking the craving in your head. You cancel the habit, yet rehearse it all day. The form changed; the attachment did not.
translationLiteral: कर्मेन्द्रियाणि (organs of action) / संयम्य (having restrained) / यः (who) / आस्ते (sits) / मनसा (with the mind) / स्मरन् (remembering, thinking of) / इन्द्रियार्थान् (sense objects) / विमूढात्मा (deluded one) / मिथ्याचारः (false actor, hypocrite) / सः (he) / उच्यते (is called)
insight: It shifts attention from outward control to inner honesty. The real task is to align thought and action together.
